DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-28-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JE NNIFER WESTMORELAND'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO 1) FORM INTERROGATORIES - EMPLOYMENT LAW, 2) FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL; 3) REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND (4) FOR SANCTIONS; THE COURT LARGELY ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE MODIFICATION THAT DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL IS TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WITH AMENDED CODE COMPLIANT RFP RESPONSES PURSUANT TO THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER WESTMORELAND'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO 1) FORM INTERROGATORIES - EMPLOYMENT LAW, 2) FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL; 3) REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION; AND FOR SANCTIONS, IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO APRIL-16-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS MATTHEW MULLENWEG, AND AUDREY HC, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS MATTHEW MULLENWEG, KATHLEEN MULLENWEG, AND AUDREY HC, LLC (COLLECTIVELY THE MULLENWEGS) MOVE TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF JENNIFFER WESTMORELAND TO SUBMIT TO AN INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION BY HIRA HANIF, M.D., A FORENSIC PSYCHIATRIST. WESTMORELAND DOES NOT OBJECT TO AN IME BUT SEEKS LIMITS ON WHAT THE MULLENWEGS PROPOSED. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS SET OUT HEREIN (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

DEFENDANT MATTHEW MULLENWEG AN INDIVIDUAL, AUDREY HC, LLC MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Off Calendar

Notice And Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS MATTHEW MULLENWEG, AND AUDREY HC, LLC's Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Of Plaintiff.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Matthew Mullenweg, Kathleen Mullenweg, and Audrey HC, LLC (collectively the Mullenwegs) move to compel plaintiff Jenniffer Westmoreland to submit to an independent medical examination by Hira Hanif, M.D., a forensic psychiatrist. Westmoreland does not object to an IME but seeks limits on what the Mullenwegs proposed. The court grants the motion as set out herein.

CCP 2032.310(a) states that a motion for mental examination must "specify the time, place, manner, conditions, scope, and nature of the examination, as well as the identity and the specialty, if any, of the person or persons who will perform the examination. The motion shall be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040." The Mullenwegs have satisfied these requirements. The court may grant the motion "only for good cause shown." (CCP 2032.320(a).) The Mullenwegs and Westmoreland agree that Westmoreland has put her mental state at issue with discovery responses indicating that she suffers ongoing mental and emotional distress. Westmoreland seeks to limit the time for the examination and objected during informal resolution discussions to detailed questioning by the examiner. Westmoreland does not object to the Houston location of the examination.

The court finds good cause here. Accordingly, the court grants the motion and orders Westmoreland to attend the in-person examination administered by Hira Hanif, M.D., on April 22, 2025 between 9 a.m. and 5:30 p.m. with a 30-minute lunch break and two additional 15-minute breaks. The examiner shall perform a standard forensic psychiatric evaluation including a mental status examination, which may include questions about medical history, social history, psychiatric history, and substance abuse history. The examiner may administer any or all of the following tests: Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory - 3 (MMPI-3); Test of Memory Malingering (TOMM); Trauma Symptom Inventory - 2 (TSI-2); Structured Interview of Reported Symptoms - 2 (SIRS-2); and Miller Forensic Assessment of Symptoms Test (M-FAST). The examination will also be limited by all of the items that the parties agreed to during their meet and confer efforts, set out at pages numbered 107-109 of Exhibit F to the Nikraftar Declaration, and limitations that are standard to these examinations: No counsel may be present; the doctor will audio record the exam; Westmoreland will not be required to fill out patient information forms; the examiner will not take photographs; Westmoreland will not be questioned about conversations with counsel or her representation; Westmoreland's counsel will receive a detailed report pursuant to CCP 2032.610; and the parties will treat the report as confidential and for use and review by attorneys and retained experts only. The Mullenwegs ask for the examination to be extended beyond 5:30 p.m. if circumstances warrant. The court declines to make the duration of the examination open-ended. In the unlikely event that extraordinary circumstances arise such that the examination cannot be completed within the allotted time, the parties can meet and confer and seek further relief from the court if needed.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion For Reasonable Atty'S Fees And Memo Of Costs On Appeal
Off Calendar Jun-28-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JENNIFER WESTMORELAND AN INDIVIDUAL Motion For Reasonable Atty'S Fees And Memo Of Costs On Appeal

Plaintiff's motion for attorney's fees is denied.

Plaintiff fails to demonstrate a statutory basis for fees since the court has yet to resolve the merits of this dispute. "[T]he 'prevailing party' in a FEHA action may be awarded reasonable attorney fees. [citations] Because FEHA does not define the term 'prevailing party,' prevailing party status is determined in this context 'based on an evaluation of whether a party prevailed 'on a practical level.'" (Bustos v. Global P.E.T., Inc. (2017) 19 Cal.App. 5th 558, 562.) "[T]he trial court must identify the prevailing party 'by analyzing the extent to which each party has realized its litigation objectives.'" (Bustos, 19 Cal.App.5th at 563.) Plaintiff has yet to achieve her litigation objectives, i.e., recover on her statutory claims. (See also Naranjo v. Spectrum Security Services, Inc. (2022) 13 Cal.5th 93, 104 [awarded fees based on the Labor Code after judgment].) Plaintiff's reference to Civil Code section 1717 in her notice of motion appears to be an error since she does not cite a contract to support her claim for fees. The court denies this premature motion.

order signed.

Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RCE)
